headline: Machine Learning Engineer | NLP & Computer Vision | MSc Computer Science, Imperial College | Python, PyTorch | Open to Work
about:
I build machine learning systems that solve real business problems at scale.

My MSc at Imperial College London focused on transformer architectures for document understanding, achieving state-of-the-art results on three benchmark datasets. Before that, I spent two years at a Series B startup shipping production ML models serving 50M+ predictions per day.

What I bring:

▸ End-to-end ML pipeline experience: data collection, feature engineering, model training, deployment, and monitoring
▸ Production NLP systems: named entity recognition, document classification, semantic search
▸ Computer vision: object detection (YOLO, Faster R-CNN), image segmentation, OCR pipelines
▸ MLOps: model versioning, A/B testing, automated retraining, drift detection

Technical stack: Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face, FastAPI, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS SageMaker, PostgreSQL.

I hold a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics from the University of Edinburgh (First Class Honours, Dean's List).

Open to: Machine Learning Engineer, NLP Engineer, Applied Scientist, and MLOps Engineer roles across the UK.

Let's connect: alex.chen.example@gmail.com

experiences.title: Machine Learning Engineer
experiences.description:
Leading the NLP team building document understanding systems for financial services clients.

▸ Designed and deployed a transformer-based document classifier processing 2M+ documents/month with 97.3% accuracy
▸ Built a semantic search engine using sentence-transformers and FAISS, reducing manual document review time by 60%
▸ Implemented automated model retraining pipeline with drift detection, reducing model degradation incidents by 80%

Stack: Python, PyTorch, Hugging Face, FastAPI, Docker, AWS

experiences.title: ML Engineering Intern
experiences.description:
Built computer vision pipelines for automated quality inspection in manufacturing.

▸ Trained YOLOv8 models for defect detection achieving 95% recall at 30 FPS on edge devices
▸ Developed data augmentation pipeline that increased training data diversity by 5x
▸ Created a Streamlit dashboard for real-time inference monitoring

educations.description:
Dissertation: Efficient Transformer Architectures for Long Document Understanding — achieved state-of-the-art results on three benchmarks using a novel sparse attention mechanism.

Key modules: Deep Learning, Natural Language Processing, Computer Vision, Probabilistic Inference, Reinforcement Learning.

First Class Honours. Dean's List all four years.

Dissertation: Convergence Properties of Stochastic Gradient Descent in Non-Convex Landscapes.

Relevant modules: Linear Algebra, Probability, Statistics, Optimisation, Numerical Methods.

projects.title: Open-Source Document OCR Pipeline
projects.description: Built an end-to-end OCR pipeline combining layout detection (LayoutLMv3), text recognition (TrOCR), and post-processing. Handles multi-column layouts, tables, and handwritten text. 500+ GitHub stars.
